mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or unemployment threshold crossings fail to predict negative returns (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during early-cycle expansions or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 310.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 310.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of observations or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of triggered months or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that directly boosts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or positive GDP growth coincides with negative equity returns in >60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate output and earnings expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive returns in >60% of cases or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward validation over any 3-year window.
